title: Radiogrammitis reducta
taxonomy: Radiogrammitis reducta belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. It is a species of terrestrial fern that is endemic to the Indo-Australian region. This species is classified under the genus Radiogrammitis, which contains other species of small to medium-sized ferns. The classification of Radiogrammitis reducta is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns that include many species found in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Radiogrammitis reducta, also known as Australian gunyah fern, is a species of fern native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Radiogrammitis reducta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.
